questions.question: What is 'Largest Contentful Paint' (LCP) measuring in Core Web Vitals?

options.text: The total number of images on the page.
How fast the main content of the page loads for the user.
How many seconds it takes to scroll to the bottom of the page.
The brightness of the website's background color.

questions.explanation: LCP tracks when the largest element (usually an image or text block) becomes visible. Google considers 2.5 seconds or less to be 'Good.'

questions.question: What is the primary function of a `sitemap.xml` file?

options.text: To provide customers with a map to your physical location.
To tell search engine crawlers about the structure and pages of your website so they can be indexed.
To store your website's database backups.
To prevent bots from visiting your site.

questions.explanation: A sitemap is like a 'Table of Contents' for robots. It ensures that every important page on your site is known to search engines, even if it has few internal links.

questions.question: A URL like `/blog/category/2023/11/posts/how-to-sell` is considered 'Bad' for what reason?

options.text: It uses too many capital letters.
It is too 'Deep,' making it harder for both users and machines to quickly understand the core topic.
Search engines are not allowed to read dates in URLs.
It contains the word 'blog' which is a banned keyword.

questions.explanation: Deeply nested URLs increase 'Click Depth' and dilute the keyword focus. 'Flat' URLs (e.g., `/how-to-sell`) are better for AEO because they signal immediate topical relevance.

questions.question: Why is 'Mobile-First Indexing' critical for solo founders to understand?

options.text: Because most founders only work from their phones.
Because apps are more important than websites in 2025.
Because Google primarily uses the mobile version of your site for ranking and indexing, even for desktop searches.
Because iPhones don't support traditional SEO.

questions.explanation: Google's index is based on mobile crawling. If your mobile site is slower, harder to navigate, or has less content than your desktop site, your overall rankings will suffer.

questions.question: What is the most effective way for a solo founder to fix slow page speeds caused by images?

options.text: Removing all images from the website.
Compressing images and converting them to modern formats like WebP.
Adding 'alt text' to every image.
Asking users to use a faster internet connection.

questions.explanation: WebP images provide superior compression compared to PNG or JPEG, significantly reducing file size without losing quality. This is the #1 technical win for site speed.

questions.question: Perform a '3-Click Audit' of your site: Can you get from your homepage to your most important 'Value' page (e.g., your course or core product) in 3 clicks or less? If not, how would you restructure your internal linking to make it easier for a machine to find that 'Value'?
promptHints: Consider adding links to your most important posts from your homepage.
Think about using a 'Main Pillar' page to connect related sub-topics.
